title: Schradera acutifolia
intro: Schradera acutifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to Southeast Asia. Traditionally, Schradera acutifolia has been used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Schradera acutifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Schradera acutifolia is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, which can be used to treat various ailments, including bronchitis, pneumonia, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Schradera acutifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Schradera and is classified as a member of the Rubiaceae family. The Rubiaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the madder family or coffee family. This classification places Schradera acutifolia within a group of plants that are primarily native to tropical regions. The plant's classification helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships and ecological roles within its native habitat.
